title: causonis japonica var. pseudotrifolia
intro: Causonis japonica var. pseudotrifolia, also known as Japanese angelica or Angelica japonica, is a plant species in the carrot family that is native to East Asia. The plant is known for its medicinal properties, which include its ability to reduce fever, relieve pain, and combat inflammation. The health benefits of Causonis japonica var. pseudotrifolia include its potential to reduce oxidative stress, improve cardiovascular health, and enhance immune function. Herbal preparations of the plant can be made into teas, infusions, or capsules, and are traditionally used to treat a range of ailments, including colds, flu, and digestive issues.
